meta.title: Unit 2 — Common & Proper Nouns (Test 10)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Final review: mastery test for common and proper nouns with capitalization.

questions.question: Which word is a proper noun?
questions.explanation: "Taipei" is a proper noun — the specific name of a city in Taiwan.

questions.question: "School" is a common noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "school" refers to any school — it's a common noun.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Halloween"? (common or proper)
questions.explanation: Halloween is a proper noun — specific holiday names must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word is a common noun?
questions.explanation: "school" is a common noun — it refers to any school.

questions.question: In a sentence, "christmas" should have a capital C.
questions.explanation: Christmas is a proper noun (holiday name) — it must always be capitalized: Christmas.

questions.question: What type of noun is "Thursday"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: Days of the week are always capitalized
questions.explanation: Thursday is a proper noun — days of the week must be capitalized.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: school/dog = general words (common). Taipei/Halloween/April = specific names (proper).

questions.question: In the sentence "Pikachu is cute and yellow," the capitalization is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "Pikachu" is a proper noun (character name) — it's correctly capitalized.

questions.question: Which sentence has correct capitalization?
options: My teacher's name is ms. chen.
My teacher's name is Ms. Chen.
My Teacher's name is Ms. Chen.
My teacher's name is Ms. chen.
questions.explanation: "Ms. Chen" is a proper noun — both the title and surname need capitals. "teacher" is common.

questions.question: What type of noun is "singer"? (common or proper)
questions.hint: It's not a specific person's name
questions.explanation: singer is a common noun — it refers to any singer, not a specific person.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Pikachu is cute and yellow." Pikachu is a proper noun.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: singer/school = general words (common). Pikachu/FamilyMart/Thursday = specific names (proper).

questions.question: "March" is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! March is a month name — a proper noun that must be capitalized.

questions.question: Which word needs a capital in this sentence? "kang chiao is our school."
questions.explanation: "Kang Chiao" is a proper noun (school name) — it needs to be capitalized.

questions.question: Fix this word: 'monday'. Write it correctly.
questions.hint: Days of the week are proper nouns
questions.explanation: Monday is a proper noun (day of the week) — it must be capitalized: Monday.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "My teacher's name is Ms. Chen." Ms. Chen is a proper noun.

questions.question: Which sentence has ALL nouns correctly capitalized?
options: We like to go to Costco.
We like to go to costco.
We Like To Go To Costco.
We like To go to Costco.
questions.explanation: Only "Costco" (a proper noun) needs a capital. The sentence is correct.

questions.question: In the sentence "A cow is cute," the word 'cow' is a proper noun.
questions.explanation: "cow" is a common noun — it refers to any cow. Common nouns don't need capitals.

questions.question: Sort these words: Common Noun or Proper Noun?
questions.explanation: beach/home = general words (common). Tuesday/Halloween/Taipei = specific names (proper).

questions.question: Fix this sentence: 'the bear's name is max.' Write the corrected name.
questions.hint: A name is a proper noun
questions.explanation: "Max" is a proper noun (a name) — it must start with a capital letter.
